questions.question: When does Vue add a reactive property as a dependency of a computed getter?
options: When the property is declared inside the component's `data()` option
Only on the first time the computed getter runs
When the property's `.value` is actually read during the getter's execution
When the property is passed as an argument to the computed function

questions.question: In a computed with an early return, if `data.value` is behind a conditional that prevents it from being read, what happens when `data` changes?
options: The computed re-evaluates immediately because Vue watches all refs in scope
The computed does not re-evaluate because `data` was not tracked in the last run
Vue throws a warning about untracked dependencies in computed properties
The computed re-evaluates but returns a stale cached value

questions.question: What is the fix for a computed property that misses dependencies due to conditional logic?
options: Replace `computed` with a `watch` that lists all dependencies explicitly
Access all dependency `.value` properties at the top of the getter before any conditionals
Add `{ immediate: true }` as a second argument to the computed call
Use `triggerRef` after each conditional block to force re-evaluation
